appraisal_events.evidence: « Ni elle d'ailleurs ni le père du narrateur ... la famille de Swann leur semblait du même genre que beaucoup d'autres familles d'agents de change ... ils parlaient d'une autre [maison] ... de financiers d'un cran supérieur à son grand-père. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The parents treat Swann’s house and family as ordinary and even point to ‘better’ examples elsewhere, deflating the narrator’s consecrated view. The narrator frames this as their lacking the special “infra-rouge” sense conferred by love.

status_effects.explanation: Within the parents’ frame, Swann’s family and house are normalized among agents de change and even ranked below others, which locally lowers his distinct social standing.
